old_claim.note: Animal Husbandry provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: Protein sequencing identifies cattle and domestic ovicaprids as the collagen sources, so livestock tissues are the required feedstock for the scoped adhesive.

source_shape.source_locator: Highlights, Abstract, and Conclusions identify cattle and domestic ovicaprid collagen and probable skin or slaughter by-products.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The detected adhesive derives from collagen-rich tissues of domestic livestock species.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Those tissues supply the biochemical material transformed into the specific glue, making managed animals a feedstock rather than general background.

ontology_before: Animal husbandry was only an optional enabler of a geographically and materially broad glue claim.
ontology_after: The Zurich adhesive is tied to the domestic species directly identified by protein sequencing.
would_reject_if: The recovered collagen peptides were reidentified as wild species or contamination unrelated to the adhesive beneath the bark strips.
short_reason: Domestic cattle and ovicaprid tissues supplied the identified collagen adhesive.
